description: Apply broad-spectrum sunscreen (SPF 30 or higher) to all exposed skin at least 20 minutes before going outside. Reapply every 2 hours or after swimming or sweating.
Wear protective clothing such as long sleeve shirts, long pants, and UV-protective fabrics (UPF 30+ or higher) to reduce skin exposure to harmful ultraviolet radiation.
Wear a wide-brim or legionnaire hat to protect the face, ears, and neck from direct sunlight.
Use UV-protective sunglasses to shield your eyes and the sensitive skin around them from harmful UV radiation.
Seek shade whenever possible, especially during peak UV hours between 10 AM and 4 PM.
Limit the amount of time spent in direct sunlight, particularly when the UV index is high or extreme.
Check the daily UV index before going outdoors and adjust your sun protection measures accordingly.
During extreme UV conditions, minimize outdoor activities and stay indoors whenever possible.
